adaptation_notes: Sample Stage 1 generation from stage1_scene_bible.txt applied to book pages 1–20 only (proof of format). Not the full film bible.

global_production_variables.directorial_treatment: 35mm anamorphic, high-contrast Milwaukee winter daylight, film grain, steady camera

Character_P.description: Adult man late 20s, thin wiry frame, short dark brown hair, thoughtful tired face; pizza-work clothes or plain t-shirt and jeans; no name tags or embroidered names on clothing.
Character_P.voice_profile: American male late 20s, Midwestern working-class, soft mid baritone, slightly breathy and tired, measured pace; same voice every scene.
Character_P.name_reveal_note: Given name withheld until late in the book; use Character_P in plans; never put personal name on clothing/UI before reveal.

Character_N.description: Older brother early 30s, tall muscular build, reddish-brown messy hair, scarred chin and left eyebrow, intimidating protective presence; gym clothes or street wear.
Character_N.voice_profile: American male early 30s, deeper baritone, confident sometimes aggressive, punchy cadence; rougher Midwestern than his brother; same voice every scene.

Character_M.description: Elderly mother mid-60s to 70s, thin frail frame, brown hair going gray, soft lined face, house clothes; moves carefully.
Character_M.voice_profile: American female mid-60s–70s, thin soft higher aged voice, slower cadence, Midwestern, gentle; consistent elderly female voice.

Character_P_Young.description: Boy about 8–9, small thin child proportions (NOT adult), short dark hair, simple t-shirt and jeans.
Character_P_Young.voice_profile: American boy ~8–9, higher light child voice not adult; curious soft; NEVER adult male voice.

Character_N_Young.description: Boy about 12, sturdy preteen (NOT adult bodybuilder), short reddish-brown hair, dirty t-shirt and jeans.
Character_N_Young.voice_profile: American preteen boy ~12, not adult baritone; confident kid energy; same in flashbacks.

Loc_Kirk_Apt.description: Fifth-floor walk-up brick apartment, downtown Milwaukee; modest, slightly tired rooms.
Loc_Kirk_Apt.visual_lock: Fifth-floor walk-up brick apartment: narrow rooms, worn furniture, kitchen window to bare winter trees, no luxury polish.

Loc_Moxie_Gym.description: Corner neighborhood gym, free weights, curl benches, fluorescent light.
Loc_Moxie_Gym.visual_lock: Neighborhood gym: fluorescent light, curl benches, chalk and iron; working-class not boutique fitness.

Loc_Kirk_Alley.description: Alley behind the building; garbage-can bases; kitchen windows facing the alley.
Loc_Kirk_Alley.visual_lock: Brick alley, dented garbage can lids as bases, kitchen window mid-height on brick house wall.

Loc_Metal_Factory.description: Local metal plant; assembly line and overhead crane.
Loc_Metal_Factory.visual_lock: Industrial interior: metal assemblies, harsh work lights, crane rails; no clean tech campus.

Loc_Milwaukee_Street.description: Downtown Milwaukee streets near Kirk / Werner.
Loc_Milwaukee_Street.visual_lock: Cold Midwestern city street, brick and asphalt, working-class storefronts.

scenes.setting: Day 1 — Kirk Street apartment / Moxie's Gym
scenes.dramatic_function: Establish brothers, Ma, Milwaukee, Nick's dominance at the gym
scenes.summary: Present-day Character_P frames life with Nick and Ma in the Kirk Street walk-up, then remembers Nick taking over Moxie's gym and curling a 50-pound warm-up while younger brother watches.
scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Overcast Milwaukee day; apartment window light then harsh gym fluorescent
scenes.wardrobe_notes: P plain casual; Nick gym-ready; Ma house clothes if seen
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

music_intent.style_description: Gritty Midwestern cinematic underscore, sparse electric guitar

story_beats.intent: Introduce Nick as feared big brother
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P present-day at apartment window; VO about real Big Brother Nick; cut to Nick's scarred face and presence making people move aside

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Big brother was mine. I'm not talking about that stupid book… No, I'm talking about my real Big Brother.

story_beats.intent: Establish home: Kirk Street fifth-floor walk-up with Ma
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P and Character_N climb/haul groceries up brick walk-up; Ma's presence in the modest apartment; cold city outside

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I lived with him and Ma after dad took off when I was about eight. We lived downtown in a big brick apartment building on Kirk Street.

story_beats.visual_event: Moxie's gym: a man drops a 40-pound preacher curl bar as Character_N approaches; Nick nods, takes the bench, loads a 50-pound warm-up bar and curls; Character_P watches, smaller, impressed
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick dominant on bench; P off-axis observer, not competing

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I'm not too shabby when it comes to weight lifting, but I ain't gonna curl no fifty pounds as a warm-up. Hell, I ain't gonna curl a 50-pound weight probably ever. That's okay though, Nick is my brother, like I said.

scenes.dramatic_function: Prove Nick's code: takes blame for Mark's broken window
scenes.summary: Kids play kickball in the alley; Mark Conner smashes Mrs. Engel's kitchen window; young Nick claims fault; Ma pays; Nick later covers the cash.
scenes.wardrobe_notes: Dirty kid clothes; child proportions only
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.intent: Kids play kickball with garbage-can bases
story_beats.visual_event: Summer alley: Character_N_Young and Character_P_Young play kickball; dented garbage can lids as bases

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We used to play kick ball in the alley behind the building and set up bases with dented-in garbage can lids.

story_beats.intent: Ball through Mrs. Engel window — fidelity: through window not over wall
story_beats.visual_event: Mark (~10) kicks red kickball; ball flies unbroken path INTO Mrs. Engel kitchen window on brick house; glass SMASHES; kids flinch

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: One time little Mark Conner kicked a ball right through old Mrs. Engel's kitchen. Man, was she pissed.

story_beats.visual_event: OTS behind Character_N_Young facing house; Mrs. Engel yells from shattered kitchen window; Nick steps toward her looking UP at window; Mark scared; Character_P_Young watches proud/scared
story_beats.blocking_notes: Camera behind young Nick; he faces house/window not camera; eyeline up to Mrs. Engel

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Mark was scared to death, but Nick stepped right up to her and said he was the one who did it.

scenes.setting: Day — Kirk apartment / city / metal factory / Nick's Trans Am
scenes.dramatic_function: Nick as modern Viking; factory job; Trans Am; care for Ma
scenes.summary: P narrates Viking ancestry pride; Nick's factory work and Werner Street life; burnt-orange Trans Am; Nick scolds P for letting Ma sleep midday.
scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Cool overcast day; industrial factory light; car exterior
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

music_intent.style_description: Low drums and cold guitar, mythic but blue-collar

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P in apartment; impressions of Nick as warrior; fire-on-water funeral image as mental image only if needed, keep grounded Milwaukee

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The thing about Nick was, he was a real-life Viking… I guess that's why people respect Nick and our family, even though dad took off.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N at metal factory assembly or crane; easy power; contrast with P's smaller world

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick has a job down at the local metal factory. He runs one of the assembly lines down there.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N's 1980 burnt-orange Pontiac Trans Am with yellow flames and hood eagle pulls up; people look; Character_P rides passenger

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It's a 1980 Pontiac Trans Am all decked out… People always look up when Nick pulls up to the lights in that car.

story_beats.visual_event: At Kirk apartment: Character_N arrives; asks why Ma is in bedroom midday; Character_P defensive; Ma half-seen; Nick lectures about old people declining
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick dominates doorway/room; P smaller; Ma not mocked

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: You got to keep an eye on her. She shouldn't be sleeping in the middle of the day like that.

scenes.dramatic_function: Ma's hard life; economic reality; P trapped as caregiver
scenes.summary: P describes Ma's janitor past, retirement, SSI, pizza money; game shows and boredom; ends-meet life of pizza and macaroni.
scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Dim apartment TV light and overcast window
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M on couch watching game shows/cooking TV; Character_P tries to engage; empty hours

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: All she wants to do is watch game shows and sometimes the cooking channels.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P in kitchen with cheap food; pizza boxes/macaroni; tired caregiver; no escape to college fantasy

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Between her and me with my pizza job money, we make ends meet. It's not always pretty. There's a lot of pizza and macaroni around here.
